mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing equity risk premium and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings expectations and lower default risk.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 payroll level 132,989k indicates ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 0.25% signals zero lower bound monetary accommodation. When FEDFUNDS remains pinned at this level, equity valuations expand due to suppressed discount rates, producing positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 FEDFUNDS at 0.14% confirms ZLB regime
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation post-2008
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during ZLB periods are not significantly positive (mean return <= 0% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity risk premium compresses and S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns through improved earnings visibility.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 HOUST at 654k and RSAFS at 408,039M indicate demand recovery
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in < 55% of cases, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5%, signaling supply-side weakness or demand destruction. This triggers negative equity returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downward and increase equity risk premium.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 INDPRO at 96.5685 reflects post-crisis industrial recovery
regime_hint: supply shock or demand contraction regime
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in < 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, signaling persistent labor market slack. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline and equity risk premium expands.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 UNRATE at 8.1% indicates elevated unemployment post-crisis
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during high UNRATE periods are not significantly negative (mean return >= 0% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates month-over-month by > 0.3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.50%, creating a negative real rate shock. This regime produces negative 20-day equity returns as investors anticipate future monetary tightening and reprrice terminal rates upward.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 CPIAUCSL at 229.177 and FEDFUNDS at 0.14% define real rate environment
regime_hint: inflation acceleration with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in < 50% of cases, or interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter is positive and accelerating (current quarter > prior quarter growth rate). This signals late-cycle expansion momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved earnings revisions and multiple expansion.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate accelerates relative to prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 GDPC1 at 13,502.4B reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during accelerating GDP periods are not significantly positive (mean return <= 0% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.06 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates while unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains above 8.0%, signaling stagflation risk. This joint condition produces negative 20-day S&P 500 returns as investors reduce equity allocations due to compressed profit margins and elevated macro uncertainty.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 INDPRO at 96.5685 and UNRATE at 8.1% define stagflation risk environment
regime_hint: stagflation risk with supply weakness and labor slack
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in < 50% of cases, or interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
